Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 11 percent year-over-year, reflecting strong performance in Amazon Web Services and advertising, partially offset by softer consumer discretionary demand in the North America and International retail segments.

Amazon Web Services revenue grew 19 percent year-over-year as demand for cloud infrastructure and artificial intelligence services accelerated, driven by enterprise digital transformation and generative AI adoption.

Advertising services revenue grew 22 percent year-over-year, the strongest growth rate in several quarters, driven by brands increasing spending on sponsored products and display formats that connect them with customers at the point of purchase intent, plus expansion of streaming video ad inventory.

International segment operating losses narrowed meaningfully as newer geographies approached profitability through cost discipline and growing Prime membership density that improves delivery economics.

We launched Trainium and Inferentia chip-powered instances in AWS to provide customers with purpose-built, cost-effective compute options for training and deploying large-scale machine learning models.

Item 3. Other Information
